requirements.text: The system shall allow users to select a specific park before entering a park-specific pass purchase flow.
requirements.annotation_notes: The flow begins with a park selection experience and then shows Great Escape selected, which directly grounds park choice as an entry step for the later purchase flow.

requirements.text: The system shall switch the user into park-specific browsing context after a park is selected.
The system shall present season pass tiers side by side with pricing and benefit information to support plan comparison.
The system shall provide a park-specific entry point for discovering available pass options.
The system shall allow the user to initiate purchase of a specific pass tier from the comparison view.
The system shall allow users to configure the quantity of passes being purchased and display information about monthly and yearly subscriptions.
requirements.annotation_notes: The Diamond pass configuration screen includes quantity controls next to the selected pass, which directly suggests quantity configuration even though only one quantity state is shown.

requirements.text: The system shall allow each purchased pass to be assigned to a named guest or passholder during checkout.
requirements.annotation_notes: The checkout flow shows a guest or passholder card and then populates it with the entered person name, grounding pass assignment to an individual.

requirements.text: The system shall collect personal information needed for passholder setup during checkout.
requirements.annotation_notes: The flow clearly shows a personal information form being filled across several steps, including fields such as name, email, zip code, and age, which grounds structured passholder data capture.

requirements.text: The system shall present multiple dining-plan variants with pricing so the user can compare optional add-ons.
requirements.annotation_notes: The add-on screen shows One Meal Dining Plan and Two Meal Dining Plan side by side with price information, directly supporting comparison among add-on variants.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve earlier user-entered personal information while the user completes the remaining required fields.
requirements.annotation_notes: Across successive form states, previously entered values remain visible while additional fields are filled, which directly grounds cross-step retention within the form.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: They are trivially preserved, since each input field stays present in the selected flow steps in the UI. Having this requirement is logically still correct, since the requirement comes before the UI. Better flow steps are the later ones, where the name isn't shown anymore, but preserved in hidden state.

requirements.text: The system shall support copying previously entered passholder information into billing-related information where appropriate.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: the age has to be entered at the beginning. then at the last step the date of birth has to be entered. this would contradict the idea of entering data once.

requirements.text: The system shall collect age or date-of-birth information when needed for pass purchase or financing eligibility.
requirements.annotation_notes: An age field appears in the personal information step, and the later agreement step references date of birth, which grounds age-related data collection even though the underlying business rule is not fully visible.

requirements.text: The system shall allow users to configure optional dining-plan add-ons as part of a season pass purchase.
requirements.annotation_notes: The checkout flow contains a dedicated add-on step for dining plans with selectable options, directly grounding add-on configuration within the purchase journey.

requirements.text: The system shall provide visible information about each offer when an add-on selection becomes active.
requirements.annotation_notes: The dining-plan step changes from an unselected to a selected state, which grounds the expectation of explicit UI feedback for active add-on choices.

requirements.text: The system shall offer a choice between full payment and a flexible installment payment plan for pass purchases.
The system shall display the installment schedule and payment amounts when a flexible payment plan is offered.
The system shall support stepwise progression through the checkout flow with consistent back and continue navigation controls.
requirements.annotation_notes: Multiple checkout stages show repeated back and continue or next controls, which grounds structured progression support across the purchase workflow.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: the steps are missing. step 7 until step 18 / 19 would all be relevant

requirements.text: The system shall present prices and option details transparently enough for users to compare pass tiers, add-ons, and payment choices during checkout.
The system shall require the user to review and consent to financing-related terms before completing a flexible payment agreement.
requirements.annotation_notes: A terms and conditions modal explicitly instructs the user to review the agreement, sign, and consent, which directly grounds agreement review as part of financing completion.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: there are no indicators in the UI that the form only has to be submitted for flexible payment agreements

requirements.text: The system shall collect billing and contact details needed to complete the payment agreement workflow.
